Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First note that events 1 3 and 13 15 have similar readings low temperature 20 21C low pressure 1 bar vibration 0 material flow 0 tool position 0 and low energy 0 4 0 5 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These likely represent idle or standby states possibly before and after the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 4 6 show a significant change temperature increases to 50 58C pressure is 1 1 5 bar vibration is 30 38 Hz material flow is 5 units s tool position increases from 5 to 10 mm and energy consumption rises to 2 3 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests an active operation High vibration and material flow might indicate cutting or machining

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 7 8 Temperature drops to 40C pressure increases to 2 bar vibration drops to 10 Hz material flow drops to 2 units s tool position is fixed at 15 mm energy is 2 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might be a different phase perhaps Assembling Parts where pressure is higher but vibration lower indicating pressing or assembling

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 9 Temperature spikes to 80C pressure remains 2 bar vibration spikes to 50 Hz material flow is 0 tool position is 15 mm energy spikes to 5 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This looks like a high energy high vibration high temperature operation with no material flow Could be Welding because welding often involves high heat and energy and no material flow as it is joining parts

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 10 12 Temperature drops to 25 30C pressure drops to 1 bar vibration is low 1 5 Hz material flow is 0 1 units s tool position is 15 20 mm energy is 1 1 6 kWh

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This seems like a cooling down or inspection phase Possibly Quality Inspection where the part is checked maybe with some movement tool position changes slightly Low energy and vibration

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So grouping Group A Events 1 3 Idle Standby Group B Events 4 6 Active operation with moderate temperature high vibration material flow Label Cutting Metal Group C Events 7 8 Lower vibration higher pressure steady state Label Assembling Parts Group D Event 9 High temperature high vibration high energy no material flow Label Welding Group E Events 10 12 Cooling down inspection like Label Quality Inspection Group F Events 13 15 Idle Standby again

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Maybe event 10 12 is Packaging But energy is low vibration low temperature low Could be packaging if it is a simple operation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Given the data I think the most reasonable grouping is Events 1 3 Idle Events 4 6 Cutting Metal Events 7 8 Assembling Parts Event 9 Welding Events 10 12 Quality Inspection Events 13 15 Idle again

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets look at event 9 welding has no material flow which makes sense because welding is joining not moving material After welding you might need to cool down and inspect Events 10 12 show cooling temperature dropping and low energy which could be inspection

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus the inferred process is Standby Cutting Metal Assembling Parts Welding Quality Inspection Standby
